title: Introduction to Functions
description: Learn to create and call functions in JavaScript
longDescription: Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform specific tasks. They are the building blocks of JavaScript programs, allowing you to organize code, avoid repetition, and create modular solutions.

learningObjectives.statement: Understand what functions are and why they're useful

missionNarrative.title: The Function Fountain
missionNarrative.story: Deep in Functions Grove lies the magical Function Fountain, where code springs to life! Learn to harness its power to create reusable spells that solve problems again and again...

sections.title: What Are Functions?
Function Declaration
💡 Pro Tip
sections.content:
**Name your functions clearly!** Use verbs that describe what the function does:

✅ `calculateTotal`, `getUser`, `validateEmail`
❌ `doStuff`, `thing`, `func1`

sections.title: Why Use Functions?
Anatomy of a Function
sections.content:
function greet(name) {        // ← function keyword + name + parameters
  return "Hello, " + name;     // ← function body + return statement
}

let message = greet("World");  // ← calling function, storing result
console.log(message);          // Output: Hello, World

challenges.title: Create Your First Function
challenges.question: Complete the function declaration
challenges.instructions: Fill in the blanks to create a working function.
challenges.successFeedback: Great job! You've created and called your first function!
challenges.failureFeedback: Remember: use 'function' to declare, and just the function name followed by () to call.

hints.text: Function declarations start with the 'function' keyword.
To call a function, write its name followed by parentheses ().

hints.finalExplanation: Use 'function introduce()' to declare, and 'introduce()' to call.

reflectionQuestion.question: Describe a task you do repeatedly in your daily life. How could you turn it into a 'function'?
reflectionQuestion.sampleAnswer: Making breakfast every morning is repetitive. As a function: function makeBreakfast() { get bread; toast bread; add butter; serve; }. I could call this function each morning!
